Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A sophisticated group known as 'FrostyGoop' targets transportation systems with a straightforward yet effective approach. They exploit public-facing applications to gain initial access, execute commands to manipulate the system, and finally impact operations by inhibiting recovery processes.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Internal Network, Malicious Payload
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Command Line Interface, Process Creation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 121; Name: Exploit Non-Production Interfaces
stage.description: An attacker identifies and exploits an unpatched vulnerability in an internet-facing application of the transportation system, allowing them unauthorized access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 160; Name: Exploit Script-Based APIs
stage.description: Once inside the system, the attacker uses a command-line interface to execute scripts and commands, enabling them to explore and manipulate the system further.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts critical system functions by creating processes that inhibit normal operation, effectively preventing recovery mechanisms from functioning properly.

Scenario.summary: The FrostyGoop group employs a streamlined yet potent strategy against transportation systems. They leverage their expertise to exploit publicly exposed services, execute harmful commands, and ultimately impair operational capabilities, causing significant disruption.
